sub_topics.title: Food and Meals
competences: Use appropriate language to talk about different foods and meals of the day

instructional_blocks.hook: Activate learners' prior knowledge by asking them to mention the food they eat at home
teacher_steps: Activate learners' prior knowledge by asking them to mention the food they eat at home
Explain that the food they have mentioned is in different types
Display the picture/chart showing the different types of food
Referring to the picture/chart, explain the types of food (fruits, vegetables, meat, grains, legumes and dairy)
Demonstrate saying the different foods found in each type
learner_steps: Mention the food they eat at home
Conduct a conversation in pairs about types of food
Practice the conversation in pairs using structure: 'Some of the … are ……'
Practice in small groups standing in a circle
Put thumbs up or down to agree/disagree with statements about food types

instructional_blocks.hook: Activate learners' prior knowledge by asking them to mention the meals that they eat at home
teacher_steps: Activate learners' prior knowledge by asking them to mention the meals that they eat at home
Explain that there are three main meals (breakfast, lunch and supper) in a day
Ask learners to share what other meals that they eat apart from the three main meals
Explain that there are also other meals known as snacks that are taken in-between the main meals
Explain the different times of day when each meal is taken
Demonstrate asking and answering questions about meals
learner_steps: Mention the meals they eat at home
Share other meals eaten apart from three main meals
Conduct a conversation in pairs about when meals are taken
Practice the conversation in pairs taking turns
Showcase dialogue in front of class
Mention the types of meals

instructional_blocks.hook: Display a picture showing people praying before having a meal
teacher_steps: Display a picture showing people praying before having a meal
Ask learners to explain what is happening in the picture
Consolidate their ideas by explaining the importance of praying before meals
Teach the learners to recite a simple prayer before meals
learner_steps: Explain what is happening in the picture
Recite the prayer after teacher as a whole class
Recite the prayer as individuals, in pairs and small groups
Recite the prayer in small groups, pairs or individuals in front of the class
Recite the prayer independently

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic emphasises on enabling learners to differentiate types of food, various meals and say a prayer before meals. Learners will use simple language to talk about different foods and meals of the day. The types of food include fruits, vegetables, meat, grains, legumes and dairy. The main meals are breakfast (taken in the morning), lunch (taken around mid-day), and supper (taken in the evening). Snacks are meals taken in-between the main meals. A simple prayer before meals is: 'Thank you God for the food, in Jesus name. Amen'

vocabulary.definition: The first meal of the day, eaten in the morning
A meal eaten around mid-day
A meal eaten in the evening
Small meals eaten in-between main meals
Type of food including mangoes, oranges, masuku
Type of food including cabbage, rape, pumpkin leaves
Type of food including rice, porridge
Type of food including beans, nuts
Type of food including milk, yoghurt
Type of food including fish, beef

questions: Ask learners to identify a food and its type
Ask learners to mention the different meals and the time of day they are taken
Ask learners to recite a prayer used before meals

sub_topics.title: Occupations
competences: Use appropriate language to talk about occupations in the community

instructional_blocks.hook: Introduce the term occupation and describe what this means
teacher_steps: Introduce the term occupation and describe what this means
Ask learners to mention occupations in the community (teachers, nurses, farmers, watchmen)
Display a picture showing different occupations
Mention the occupations shown in the picture
Ask learners to mention the occupations shown in the picture
Demonstrate how to converse about occupations in the community
learner_steps: Mention occupations in the community
Mention occupations shown in the picture
Conduct conversations in pairs or small groups using picture cards
Practice the conversation with different partners
Showcase dialogue in front of class
Mention different occupations in the community

instructional_blocks.activity_title: Stating the role played by people in different occupations
instructional_blocks.hook: Display the chart showing different occupations in the community
teacher_steps: Display the chart showing different occupations in the community
Ask learners to mention the different occupations shown in the picture
Discuss the roles of people in different occupations in the community
Demonstrate pair work about what different occupations do
learner_steps: Mention different occupations shown in the picture
Conduct a conversation in pairs about what occupations do
Practice with different partners
Present practiced conversation to whole class
Pick picture card and say occupation and what it does

instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to mention the different roles played by people in various occupations
teacher_steps: Ask learners to mention the different roles played by people in various occupations
Explain to the learners that they will play roles for different occupations in the community
Demonstrate the role of an occupation (e.g., carpenter planing wood)
learner_steps: Mention different roles played by people in various occupations
Conduct conversations in pairs or small groups pretending to do occupation actions
Practice with different partners taking turns
Present role-play and conversation to whole class
Respond to teacher pointing at occupations on chart

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on equipping learners with the ability to use appropriate language to talk about occupations in the community. Learners will explore and practice words commonly used when talking about occupations in the community. An occupation is the work or job that a person does. Examples include: teachers (teach people), nurses (help sick people), farmers (grow food), carpenters (make chairs, tables), police (protect people), drivers (drive vehicles). This topic is important as it provides learners with opportunities to explore, build, and apply appropriate vocabulary in different contexts.

vocabulary.definition: The work or job that a person does
A person who teaches people
A person who helps sick people
A person who grows food
A person who makes chairs, tables and other wooden items
A person who protects people
A person who drives vehicles

questions: Ask learners to name different occupations in their community
Ask learners to say different roles that are played by different occupations in the community
Ask learners to role-play appropriate actions that different occupations do

sub_topics.title: Things found in a home
competences: Use appropriate language to talk about things found at home

instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to describe their house (bring out the number of rooms)
teacher_steps: Ask learners to describe their house (bring out the number of rooms)
Explain that different rooms make the parts of a house
Ask learners to mention the parts of a house (kitchen, bedroom, toilet, bathroom, sitting room)
Explain the uses of each part of the house (a bedroom is for sleeping)
Demonstrate a conversation on parts of the house and their use
learner_steps: Describe their house
Mention the parts of a house
Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'Where do we …?'
Practice dialogue in pairs about where activities happen
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Perform dialogue in front of class
Name parts of a house and their uses

instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to name things found in their home
teacher_steps: Ask learners to name things found in their home (plates, spoon, sofas/chairs, television, tables, beds, blankets)
Display a poster showing the parts of a house and different things found in each part
Identify the different things from each part of the house
Ask learners to mention other things and the part of the house where they are found
Demonstrate a conversation on things found in a home and where they are found
learner_steps: Name things found in their home
Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'Where is the …?'
Practice dialogue in pairs about where things are found
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Present dialogue in front of class
Name things found in a home and where they are found

instructional_blocks.activity_title: Uses of things found in a home
instructional_blocks.hook: Revise things found in a home and where they are found
teacher_steps: Revise things found in a home and the part of the house where they are found
Using a chart with different types of things found in a home, discuss the use of each thing
Ask learners to mention other things found in a home and their use
Demonstrate a conversation on uses of things found in a home
learner_steps: Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'We use'
Practice dialogue in pairs (one to start, one to finish)
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Stand in two lines to complete sentences about uses
Mention the use of things found in a home

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on equipping learners with the ability to use appropriate language to discuss things found in a home. Learners will name parts of a house, things found in a home and their uses. Different rooms make the parts of a house including: kitchen (for cooking), bedroom (for sleeping), toilet, bathroom (for bathing), sitting room (for sitting/watching TV). Things found in different parts include: Kitchen - spoons, pans, plates; Bedroom - bed, blanket; Sitting room - sofas/chairs, television, tables. Uses of things: Spoons for eating, pots for cooking, bed for sleeping, chairs for sitting, soap for bathing.

vocabulary.definition: A room used for cooking
A room used for sleeping
A room used for bathing
A room used for sitting and relaxation
A room with a facility for disposal of human waste

questions: Ask learners to name the parts of a house and their uses
Ask learners to identify things in a home and where they are found
Ask learners to mention the use of things found at home

sub_topics.title: Things found at school
competences: Use appropriate language to talk about things found at school

instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to identify things found in the classroom
teacher_steps: Ask learners to identify things found in the classroom
Tell learners to point and say the names of things found in the classroom
Demonstrate how to ask and respond correctly: 'What is this?' 'This is a chair, table, desk'
learner_steps: Identify things found in the classroom
Point and say names of things in classroom
Participate in whole class choral repetition of 'What is this?'
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Role-play scenarios in small groups
Perform dialogue in front of class
Name things found in classroom when teacher points

instructional_blocks.activity_title: Uses of things found in a classroom
instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to mention things found in the classroom
teacher_steps: Ask learners to mention things found in the classroom
Display a chart showing different things found in the classroom or real objects
Discuss the use of things on the chart
Demonstrate how to ask and respond correctly using pictures on the chart
learner_steps: Mention things found in the classroom
Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'We use'
Practice dialogue in pairs (one to start, one to finish)
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Stand in two lines to complete sentences
Point at items and mention name and use

instructional_blocks.hook: Display a chart showing how we keep our classroom clean
teacher_steps: Display a chart showing how we keep our classroom clean
Tell learners to say what is happening in the picture
Discuss how to keep the classroom clean with learners (sweeping, mopping, dusting, shining, throwing litter in the bin)
Demonstrate how to ask and respond correctly
learner_steps: Say what is happening in the picture
Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'we should… the classroom?'
Practice dialogue in pairs about keeping classroom clean
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Role-play scenarios in small groups
Perform play in front of class
State at least one way of keeping the classroom clean

instructional_blocks.hook: Introduce the phrase 'things found at school'
teacher_steps: Introduce the phrase 'things found at school'
Explain and give examples (playground, library, field, classroom, garden, office, flag pole, bathrooms)
Display a chart showing things found at school
Demonstrate how to ask and respond correctly: 'What is this?' 'This is a playground'
learner_steps: Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'This is a …'
Practice dialogue in pairs asking 'what is this?' and responding
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Practice in small groups
Present dialogue to class
Mention at least one thing found at school

instructional_blocks.activity_title: Uses of things found at school
instructional_blocks.hook: Revise the things found at school
teacher_steps: Revise the things found at school
Display a chart showing things found at school (playgrounds, swings, taps, flag, library, playparks, flowers, trees, bins)
Explain the use of things found at school
Demonstrate how to ask and respond correctly
learner_steps: Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'We use... for...'
Practice dialogue in pairs or small groups
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Role-play scenarios in small groups
Present dialogue to class
Mention use of one thing found at school

instructional_blocks.hook: Prepare school surroundings to be in a mess and ask 'Look at our school. What is wrong?'
teacher_steps: Recap on the things found at school
Prepare the school surrounding to be in a mess with dirt/rubbish and leaves/litters
Tell the learners: 'Look at our school. What is wrong?'
Let the learners pick the dirt and put the space in order
Explain the importance of keeping the school clean
Display a conversation poster showing things done to keep the school clean
Demonstrate the conversation on how to keep the school clean
learner_steps: Pick dirt and put space in order
Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'What is he/she doing?'
Practice conversation in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Role-play scenarios in small groups
Perform activities in front of class
Sing song about keeping school clean

instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to mention what they like doing at school
teacher_steps: Recap on keeping the school clean
Ask learners to mention what they like doing at school
Display a conversation poster showing different activities done at school
Demonstrate the conversation about what learners like doing at school
learner_steps: Mention what they like doing at school
Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'What do you like doing?'
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Role-play scenarios in small groups
Perform activity in front of class
Sit down when teacher mentions activity they like

instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to mention games they like playing
teacher_steps: Recap on the phrase 'What do you like doing?'
Ask learners to mention games they like playing
Display a conversation poster showing different games
Introduce the words: hop, jump, skip, kick, march, catch, run
Demonstrate the conversation on games learners like playing
learner_steps: Mention games they like playing
Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'What games do you like?'
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Play games they like in small groups
Perform activity in front of class
Play 'Simon says' game as whole class

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on equipping learners with the ability to use appropriate language to talk about things found at school. Things found in a classroom include: desks, chairs, tables, pencils, books, chalkboard, crayons, rulers. Uses: pencils for writing, desks for sitting, books for writing, crayons for coloring, rulers for measuring. Ways to keep classroom clean: sweeping, mopping, dusting, shining, throwing litter in bin. Things found at school: playground, library, field, classroom, garden, office, flag pole, bathrooms, swings, taps. Uses: library for keeping books/reading, bins for throwing litter, swings for playing, taps for drinking water. Activities done at school: reading, writing, playing, singing, drawing, eating. Games: hop, jump, skip, kick, march, catch, run. Ways to keep school clean: slashing, sweeping, mopping, picking litter, weeding.

vocabulary.definition: A piece of furniture used for sitting while writing or reading
A board used by teachers for writing with chalk
An area at school where children play
A place where books are kept for reading
To jump on one foot
To move by hopping on alternate feet
To walk with regular steps

questions: Ask learners to mention things found in the classroom
Ask learners to mention the uses of things found in the classroom
Ask learners to mention ways of keeping the classroom clean
Ask learners to mention things found at school
Ask learners to mention the use of things found at school
Ask learners to mention things that they like doing at school
Ask learners to mention games they like playing at school

sub_topics.title: Domestic animals
competences: Use appropriate language to describe domestic animals

instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to mention animals they keep at home
teacher_steps: Ask learners to mention animals they keep at home
Introduce the phrase 'domestic animals' as animals we keep at home
Display a conversation poster showing different types of domestic animals (pig, dog, cat, cow, goat, hen, duck, pigeon, guinea fowl)
Demonstrate the conversation about domestic animals
learner_steps: Mention animals they keep at home
Conduct whole class choral repetition of 'No, it is not. Yes, it is'
Practice dialogue in pairs identifying domestic animals
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Practice in small groups
Present dialogue to class
Play game identifying animals by their sounds

instructional_blocks.hook: Recap on the names of different domestic animals
teacher_steps: Recap on the names of the different domestic animals
Identify the sounds that different domestic animals make
Display a conversation poster showing different domestic animals
Ask learners to say the colour or size of different animals on chart
Demonstrate the conversation describing domestic animals by colour and size
learner_steps: Identify sounds of domestic animals
Say colour or size of animals on chart
Conduct whole class choral repetition describing animals
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Practice in small groups
Present dialogue to class
Sing song describing domestic animals

instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to explain the uses of animals they keep at home
teacher_steps: Recap on 'describing domestic animals'
Ask learners to explain the uses of animals they keep at home
Display a conversation poster showing domestic animals
Explain the different uses for domestic animals (food - goat, transport - cow, labour - cow, security - dogs)
Demonstrate the conversation on the uses of domestic animals
learner_steps: Explain uses of animals kept at home
Conduct whole class choral repetition on uses
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Practice in small groups
Present dialogue to class
Sing song about domestic animals

instructional_blocks.activity_title: Food we get from domestic animals
instructional_blocks.hook: Review the uses of domestic animals
teacher_steps: Review the uses of domestic animals
Explain that we get food from different domestic animals
Mention the food we get from animals (Meat, milk, eggs, sausage, butter, yogurt, cheese, oil)
Ask learners to mention other foods we get from animals
Demonstrate talking about food we get from domestic animals
learner_steps: Practice whole class talking about food from animals
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Look at pictures and identify foods
Present dialogue practiced
Sing song on food from animals

instructional_blocks.hook: Arrange desks with pictures showing homes of four domestic animals
teacher_steps: Arrange desks in classroom with pictures of animal homes
Tell learners to identify which domestic animal lives in each home
Explain why domestic animals need homes
Talk about homes for domestic animals (Dog house, goat house, barn, shed, cage, pond)
Demonstrate talking about homes for domestic animals
learner_steps: Identify which animals live in each home
Practice talking about homes for animals
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Practice in small groups
Look at pictures and present dialogue
Role-play animals and locate their home

instructional_blocks.hook: Introduce the word 'care' and explain it means being kind and helping others
teacher_steps: Introduce the word 'care'
Explain that 'care' is being kind and helping others
Give example: When you feed domestic animals, you are taking care of them
Demonstrate talking about taking care of domestic animals
learner_steps: Practice conversation about caring for animals
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Practice in small groups with picture flashcards
Pick flashcards and say way of caring shown
Share dialogue on flashcards picked
Sing song on taking care of animals

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on equipping learners with knowledge about domestic animals. Domestic animals are animals we keep at home and they are important as they contribute to food security, economic benefits and environmental sustainability. Types of domestic animals include: pig, dog, cat, cow, goat, hen, duck, pigeon, guinea fowl. Animals can be described by colour and size. Uses of domestic animals: food (goat, chicken), transport (cow), labour (cow), security (dogs). Food we get from animals: meat, milk, eggs, sausage, butter, yogurt, cheese, oil. Homes of animals: dog house, goat house, barn, shed, cage, pond. Ways to care for animals: give them food, water, medicine, home.

vocabulary.definition: Animals we keep at home
Being kind and helping others
A large building where cows and other farm animals are kept
A small building used for storing things or keeping animals
A structure made of bars where birds or small animals are kept
A small area of water where fish and ducks can live

questions: Ask learners to identify the different domestic animals
Ask learners to describe the size and colour of different domestic animals
Ask learners to mention the uses of domestic animals
Ask learners to mention the foods we get from domestic animals
Ask learners to mention the homes of different domestic animals
Ask learners to mention ways of caring for domestic animals

sub_topics.title: At the Market
competences: Use appropriate language for buying and selling

instructional_blocks.activity_title: Identify items found at the market
instructional_blocks.hook: Introduce the word 'market' as a place where people buy and sell things
teacher_steps: Introduce the word 'market' and explain it's a place where people buy and sell things
Display a chart showing some items found at the market
Tell learners to identify items from the chart (salt, chicken, soap, rice, maize, oil, vegetables, tomatoes, onions, fish, fruits, clothes, shoes)
Ask learners to mention more items found at the market
Demonstrate having a conversation about items found at the market
learner_steps: Identify items from chart
Mention more items found at market
Practice dialogue in pairs naming items
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Visit market stand and ask/respond about items
Identify items found at market

instructional_blocks.activity_title: Identify people found at the market
instructional_blocks.hook: Ask learners to mention people found at the market
teacher_steps: Revise the items found at the market
Ask learners to mention people found at the market (buyers, sellers, vendors, porters, guards)
Ask learners to mention more people found at the market
Explain what we mean by buyers and sellers
Display a chart showing people buying and selling
Tell learners to identify people on the chart, who is buying and who is selling
Demonstrate the conversation on who is buying and selling at the market
learner_steps: Mention people found at market
Practice dialogue in pairs naming people from chart
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Present dialogue to class
Mention people found at market

instructional_blocks.hook: Explain that buying is giving seller money for item and selling is getting money from buyer
teacher_steps: Review the phrases 'buying and selling'
Explain that buying is giving the seller money for the item and selling is getting money from the buyer for the item
Display a chart showing items and their prices
Demonstrate the conversation used for buying and selling
learner_steps: Practice dialogue in pairs buying and selling
Rotate pairs to have turn at buying and selling
Visit market stand and present dialogue
Mention language used when buying and selling

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on equipping learners with the ability to use appropriate language for buying and selling at the market. A market is a place where people buy and sell things. Items found at the market include: salt, chicken, soap, rice, maize, oil, vegetables, tomatoes, onions, fish, fruits, clothes, shoes. People found at the market include: buyers (people who buy things), sellers (people who sell things), vendors, porters, guards. Language for buying and selling includes asking 'What is this?', 'How much is it?', 'May I have one?', and responding with item names and prices.

vocabulary.definition: A place where people buy and sell things
A person who buys things
A person who sells things
A person who sells goods at the market
Giving the seller money for the item
Getting money from the buyer for the item

questions: Ask learners to identify the items at a market
Ask learners to mention the people found at the market
Ask learners to mention the language used to buy and sell at the market

sub_topics.title: Community practices
competences: Use appropriate language to talk about community practices

instructional_blocks.hook: Explain that community practices is when people work together to help everyone
teacher_steps: Introduce the phrases 'community and practices'
Explain that community practices is when people work together to help everyone
Use a poster or chart showing people working together (sweeping, sharing, planting trees)
Ask learners to mention good community practices including others not on the chart
Demonstrate the conversation used to talk about good community practices
learner_steps: Practice conversations in pairs or small groups
Supervise to ensure everyone practices
Present dialogue in front of class
Mention good community practices

instructional_blocks.activity_title: How we live together in harmony
instructional_blocks.hook: Explain that harmony is when people work together to make something better
teacher_steps: Introduce the word 'harmony'
Explain that harmony is when people work together to make something better
Describe how we live together in harmony
Explain that we work together in harmony by sharing work fairly, supporting each other, appreciating, respecting and tolerating each other
Display a poster showing people living in harmony (kind, sharing, caring, honest)
Explain each picture and how the action relates to living in harmony
Demonstrate talking about how we live together in harmony
learner_steps: Practice conversations in pairs or small groups
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Create role-play in small groups
Present to class
Mention words describing living in harmony

instructional_blocks.hook: Explain that community is a group of people who live, work and help each other
teacher_steps: Revise the word 'community'
Explain that a community is a group of people who live, work and help each other
Ask learners to mention how they keep the community clean
Demonstrate the conversation on how to keep the community clean
learner_steps: Mention how to keep community clean
Practice dialogue in pairs
Rotate pairs to practice with different partners
Go to school grounds in small groups
Demonstrate one way to keep community clean
Mention ways to keep community clean

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on equipping learners with the ability to use appropriate language to talk about community practices. Community practices is when people work together to help everyone. Good community practices include: sweeping, sharing, planting trees, helping each other. Harmony is when people work together to make something better. We live together in harmony by: sharing work fairly, supporting each other in chores, appreciating each other, respecting each other, tolerating each other. We can describe living in harmony with words like: kind, fair, honest, caring, sharing, helping. Community is a group of people who live, work and help each other. Ways to keep community clean: slashing, putting litter in bin, sweeping, weeding, picking rubbish.

vocabulary.definition: A group of people who live, work and help each other
Activities or behaviors that people do regularly
When people work together to make something better
Being kind and helping others

questions: Ask learners to mention some good community practices
Ask learners to mention how people live together in harmony
Ask learners to mention ways of keeping the community clean
